    Jason A. Yoder

    Assistant Professor of CSSE at Rose-Hulman Institute of Technology. Researching bio-inspired AI, neuroevolution, and evolutionary development.

    • GitHub
    • Google Scholar
    • Email

    Personal

    On this page

    • Sports & Hobbies

    Sports & Hobbies

    I’m an avid mover. Beyond ultimate frisbee (see Coaching), I enjoy pickleball, paddleboarding, rock-climbing, backpacking, bicycling, mountain biking, table tennis, snowboarding, basketball, tennis, weightlifting, soccer, badminton, roller hockey, ice hockey, volleyball, water skiing, wakeboarding, longboarding, rollerblading, and unicycling.

    Games

    I’m a board game enthusiast—my favorites include 4X, worker placement, engine building, cooperative, RPG, and strategy games. I also enjoy computer games (puzzle, RTS, RPG) and have programmed text-based games to teach Python.

    Reading & Podcasts

    I’ve read books spanning personal productivity, evolution and development, theory of mind and consciousness, leadership, and science fiction. Some favorites:

    • What is Intelligence? by Blaise Aguera y Arcas
    • Antifragile by Nassim Nicholas Taleb
    • Sapiens by Yuval Noah Harari
    • I Am a Strange Loop by Douglas Hofstadter
    • How to Change Your Mind by Michael Pollan
    • A Thousand Brains by Jeff Hawkins
    • Range by David Epstein
    • Drive by Daniel Pink
    • Diaspora and Permutation City by Greg Egan

    Podcasts I follow: Lex Fridman, Brain-Inspired, and Optimal Living Daily.

    Service & Awards

    Service

    • Chair, Rose-Hulman Diversity Council (2020–2022): Led “Creating Inclusive Courses” initiative
    • CSSE Diversity Committee Member (2019–present)
    • CSSE Academic Integrity Committee (2024)
    • Laxer International Travel Grant Committee (2020–present)
    • CSSE Placement Exam primary contact (2023–present)
    • GitHub for Education organizer for RHIT (2020–present)
    • Senior Project Client for RoseData (2019–present) and Computational Experiment Pipeline (2021–present)
    • CSSE Research Symposium co-organizer with Robert Williamson (first ever, 2024)
    • Conference Reviewer: ALIFE 2021, 2023
    • President, Timber Ridge HOA, Bloomington, IN (2019–2021)

    Awards

    Award Year
    NSF Research Opportunity Award (~$24,000) 2021
    Indiana University Male Big of the Year Award 2019
    Cognitive Science Supplemental Research Fellowship 2015, 2017, 2018
    Sarah D. Barder Fellowship, Johns Hopkins CTY 2017
    Associate Instructor of the Year, IU School of Informatics 2016
    Graduated summa cum laude, Goshen College 2009
    Maple Scholars Summer Research Program 2008
    SMART Grant (Federal Government, $4,000) 2007, 2008
    Presidential Leadership Award ($10,000, Goshen College) 2004–2008
    Gatorade Student Athlete Scholarship ($5,000, Tennis) 2004
    Valedictorian, Southmoreland High School 2004

    Professional Development

    • Completed CornellX “Teaching & Learning in the Diverse Classroom” (April 2023)
    • BioInformatics Reading Group with Robert Williamson (2019–2021)
    • Regular participant in IU Evolutionary and Adaptive Systems Lab meetings (2020–present)

    Conferences Attended

    ALIFE (2014, 2018, 2020–2025), GECCO (2022, 2023), CUR (2019, 2021–2023), CSEE&T 2020, CogSci 2020, Sarah D. Barder Conference (2017, 2021, 2022), IEEE SSCI 2017
